title: Why 64% of NMN Supplements Contain Zero Active Ingredient
description: Independent lab tests exposed the NMN market. Here is how to spot the real ones in 90 seconds.

title: Omega-3: The One Supplement Almost Everyone Is Taking Wrong
description: Form matters more than dose. Most cheap fish oil fails before it reaches you.

title: Vitamin D3 + K2: Why the Experts Can't Agree on a Number
description: The Endocrine Society reversed its own 30-year-old thresholds in 2024. The IOM still says 600 IU is plenty. Finland mandates fortification and nearly eliminated deficiency; the UK fortifies almost nothing. Here is the real, contested picture.

title: Magnesium: The Master Mineral That Everyone's Missing
description: Roughly half the US population doesn't get enough magnesium, and standard blood tests miss most of it. The 'glycinate for sleep, threonate for brain' story you've heard is largely marketing - no trial has ever compared the forms head-to-head. Here is what the actual research supports, form by form.

title: Zinc: The Quiet Mineral That Keeps Everything Running
description: Subtle deficiency quietly erodes immunity, testosterone and mental sharpness.

title: Selenium: Small Amounts, Big Impact - If You Actually Need It
description: A $114 million trial of 35,533 men was stopped early after a possible signal linking selenium to higher diabetes risk. The explanation isn't that selenium is dangerous - it's that the men were already selenium-replete, and there's a real, mouse-confirmed mechanism for why excess matters. Here is the full, honest picture.

title: CoQ10: The Heart Evidence Is Strong, the Muscle Evidence Is Mixed
description: Statins lower CoQ10 levels - that part is settled biochemistry. Whether supplementing reverses statin-related muscle pain is genuinely unsettled: a 2025 meta-analysis found 4 of 7 trials positive, 3 found nothing. The heart failure evidence is considerably stronger. Here is what actually holds up.

title: Vitamin C: The Buffered Backbone of Antioxidant Defense
description: Regenerates Vitamin E and glutathione. One of the highest evidence-to-cost ratios in supplements.

title: PQQ: The Mitochondrial Spark That Keeps You Young and Sharp
description: Most supplements improve what you have. PQQ helps you grow new mitochondria.

title: Resveratrol: The Red Wine Molecule That Still Has Real Potential
description: Activates your longevity genes. Mimics caloric restriction. But only works in the right stack - here is what the science actually says.

title: Quercetin: The Swiss Army Knife of Flavonoids
description: Antioxidant, zinc ionophore, senolytic assistant and anti-inflammatory - all in one molecule. One of the most versatile flavonoids in the longevity toolkit.

title: Pterostilbene: The Sleek Cousin of Resveratrol With Extra Staying Power
description: 80% bioavailability vs 20% for resveratrol. 7x longer half-life. Better brain penetration. But there is a catch with LDL - here is the full picture.

title: NR (Nicotinamide Riboside): The NAD+ Precursor With the Longest Track Record
description: NR has 40+ human clinical trials and a 2026 head-to-head study showing 2.3x more NAD+ than NMN at the same dose. The most evidence-backed NAD+ precursor available.

title: Stack Audit: The Supplement Combinations You Can Stop Buying
description: Most people are doubling up on supplements that hit the same targets. ARI breaks down which combinations are redundant - and where to spend your budget instead.

title: Spermidine: The Autophagy Activator That Mimics Fasting
description: Spermidine triggers the same cellular cleanup as fasting. A 2024 Nature study confirmed it is the essential mediator of fasting's longevity effects.

title: Alpha-Lipoic Acid: The Dual-Soluble Antioxidant with Mitochondrial Muscle
description: ALA works in water AND fat - reaching mitochondria and the brain where other antioxidants cannot. A 2025 meta-analysis of 63 RCTs confirms its metabolic benefits.

title: Glycine: The Sweet Amino Acid That Calms, Repairs, and Rebuilds
description: 3g before bed improves sleep from night one. Combined with NAC, it reversed 7 of 9 aging hallmarks in a clinical trial. One of the best-value longevity supplements available.

title: Acetyl-L-Carnitine: Strong for Depression and Nerve Pain, Genuinely Unsettled for Dementia
description: ALCAR has solid 2025 evidence for depression and established support for peripheral neuropathy. For dementia, Cochrane says evidence doesn't justify routine use, while other meta-analyses find modest benefit - a genuine, unresolved disagreement.

title: Creatine: The Muscle Molecule That's Actually a Brain Booster in Disguise
description: A 2025 trial slowed Alzheimer's cognitive decline by 30% with creatine. One of the safest, cheapest and most studied supplements - and most people are taking it for entirely the wrong reasons.

title: Collagen: Rebuilding You from the Inside Out
description: A 2024 RCT with 112 women confirmed significant skin improvements in 8 weeks. But which type do you need - Type I, II or III? And why vitamin C is non-negotiable.

title: Sulforaphane: The Detox Whisperer That Flips Your Genetic Switches
description: Sulforaphane activates 200+ protective genes through NRF2. A 12-month trial reduced lung cancer risk markers. But most supplements deliver almost none - here is why and what to look for.

title: The Biohacker's Coffee: What the Science Actually Says About Upgrading Your Morning Cup
description: Coconut oil for ketones. Ceylon cinnamon for blood sugar. Raw cacao for focus. Collagen for structure. The evidence behind every ingredient in an optimized morning coffee.

title: My Personal Stack: How a 48-Year-Old Nutritionist Built His Daily Protocol
description: Not just what to take - but how to build a protocol that fits your life. Research, redundancy checks, timing and the practical systems that make consistency automatic.

title: The Foundation Stack: The Three Supplements Almost Everyone Should Be Taking
description: D3+K2, Magnesium and Omega-3. A 2025 Nature Aging study found they slow DNA methylation clocks with additive benefits. The highest-impact starting point for any longevity protocol.

title: The Anti-Aging Stack: NMN/NR, Resveratrol, TMG, CoQ10 and PQQ
description: Five compounds targeting the NAD+ pathway from different angles. NMN fuels sirtuins, resveratrol activates them, CoQ10 runs the electron transport chain, PQQ builds new mitochondria - and TMG prevents the hidden methylation cost most people miss.

title: The Sleep Stack: Magnesium Glycinate, Ashwagandha and L-Theanine
description: Three compounds addressing the three root causes of poor sleep: magnesium deficiency, elevated evening cortisol and neural over-excitation. No grogginess. Better sleep architecture.

title: The Brain Stack: Omega-3, Lion's Mane, PQQ and CDP-Choline
description: CDP-choline improved memory in a 100-person RCT. Lion's Mane stimulates NGF. Omega-3 is a structural brain component. PQQ builds new mitochondria in neurons. Four targets, one protocol.

title: The Vitamin D Conspiracy: How One Vitamin Exposes the Trillion-Dollar System
description: Why do official guidelines use doses studies show are inadequate? The VITAL study reported no benefit - but used sub-therapeutic doses. The book that explains why this keeps happening. 300+ scientific sources.

title: The $1,000 Longevity Plan: Add 15 Healthy Years by Choice, Not Chance
description: 68 supplements analyzed. Blue Zone nutrition. Daily protocols for real people. 70-80% of your longevity is not genetics - it is choices. Here is the map.

title: Ashwagandha: The Stress Adaptogen with a Safety Warning Worth Reading
description: Strong evidence for cortisol and stress reduction. Also: 23 documented liver injury cases, thyroid interference, and UK regulatory concern in 2024. The complete picture - including who should not take it.

title: L-Theanine: Calm Focus Without Sedation
description: L-theanine promotes alpha brain waves within 30-40 minutes - calm alertness without sedation. A 2024 28-day RCT confirmed stress and sleep benefits. One of the safest supplements available.

title: Lion's Mane: The NGF-Stimulating Mushroom for Brain Health and Neuroplasticity
description: Lion's Mane stimulates NGF - the protein responsible for neuron survival and growth. A 2025 double-blind RCT confirmed cognitive improvements in healthy adults. Form matters enormously - here is what to look for.

title: CDP-Choline (Citicoline): The Brain's Most Bioavailable Choline Source
description: 500mg/day improved episodic memory in a 100-person RCT. CDP-choline delivers choline for acetylcholine AND cytidine for synaptic membranes - making it more complete than any other choline supplement.

title: Reishi: The Immortality Fungus That Actually Has Science Behind It
description: Ganoderma lucidum is the most studied medicinal mushroom on earth. Ganoderic acids, beta-glucans, telomere preservation, and a 2024 RCT. Here is what the science actually shows.

title: Cordyceps: The Energy Mushroom With Actual Performance Data
description: Cordyceps activates AMPK, boosts mitochondrial biogenesis, and has real VO2max data from human RCTs. Here is what the science shows - and why species selection matters more than dose.

title: Chaga: The Antioxidant Powerhouse With a Real Immune and Liver Story
description: Betulinic acid, melanin, and a gut microbiome finding nobody talks about. Here is what the Chaga research actually shows - and why the ORAC score claim is mostly meaningless.

title: Maitake: The Blood Sugar Mushroom With a Vitamin D Secret
description: A real human dose-escalation immune trial, genuine blood sugar effects, and an underrated vitamin D2 pathway. Here is what the Maitake science actually shows.

title: Turkey Tail: The Only Mushroom With an Actual Approved Cancer Drug Inside It
description: PSK has been a government-approved adjuvant cancer therapy in Japan for 30+ years, backed by large RCTs. Here is the strongest clinical evidence base of any medicinal mushroom - and where it does not apply.

title: The Mushroom Protocol: Six Medicinal Mushrooms, One Coherent Stack
description: Reishi, Cordyceps, Chaga, Turkey Tail, Maitake and Lion's Mane each target a different system. Here is how to combine them without redundancy - dosing, timing and which ones you actually need.

title: Curcumin: The Anti-Inflammatory With a Bioavailability Problem
description: 21 trials, 1,705 patients confirm curcumin lowers inflammatory markers in osteoarthritis. But the black pepper trick may not work the way you think. Here is the real evidence.

title: Shilajit: The Himalayan Tar With a Heavy Metal Problem
description: A 90-day RCT shows real testosterone gains. Fulvic acid content varies 32,000% between commercial products. Here is what shilajit actually does - and why third-party testing is not optional.

title: The Dangerous Supplements Nobody Warns You About
description: Lead chromate in turmeric. Counterfeit saffron. Hidden pharmaceutical drugs in muscle-building products. Here is what is actually getting into your supplements - and how to check.

title: Fisetin: The Senolytic Flavonoid With Real Human Trial Data
description: A Mayo Clinic trial in long-COVID patients. A phase 2 sepsis trial enrolling now. Fisetin has more active human research than almost any other senolytic - and a real bioavailability gap nobody mentions.

title: TMG: The Methyl Donor Every NMN User Forgets to Take
description: TMG reliably lowers homocysteine and replenishes methyl groups depleted by NAD+ precursors. But higher doses raise LDL, and the cardiovascular benefit everyone assumes has never been confirmed.

title: Do Supplements Even Work? What We Found Out the Hard Way
description: Most supplements are marketing dressed up as science. A few have evidence as strong as anything in medicine. The skeptics are not wrong about the industry - they are wrong that the question has one answer.

title: Berberine: The 'Nature's Ozempic' Claim, Fact-Checked
description: Social media calls it 'nature's Ozempic.' The blood sugar data is real and surprisingly strong. The weight loss comparison is where the hype runs ahead of the evidence.

title: Astaxanthin: The Algae Antioxidant That Protects From the Inside Out
description: One of the most potent antioxidants ever measured in lab assays. A 2026 RCT confirmed real UV protection in human skin at 6mg daily - here is what the data actually shows.

title: Papain: The Papaya Enzyme With a Real FDA Warning Behind It
description: Papain has decades of use as a digestive aid and wound-care agent - and a 2008 FDA market withdrawal for topical use due to allergy risk. Here is what the evidence actually supports and where the real caution lies.

title: Bromelain: The Pineapple Enzyme With Actual RCTs Behind It
description: Bromelain has real randomized controlled trials behind it - pediatric sinusitis, ulcerative colitis, periodontal surgery. It also has a documented bleeding risk that matters more than most supplement warnings.

title: Supplement Interactions Nobody Explains Properly
description: Interaction checkers disagree with each other and none of them explain why. Here are the supplement-drug interactions that actually matter, organized by mechanism instead of as a list to memorize.

title: Vitamin K2 (MK-7): The Calcium Traffic Controller Almost Nobody Takes
description: A 2026 JAMA Cardiology trial found MK-7 slowed coronary artery calcification by nearly a third over two years. It also interferes with warfarin at doses as low as 10mcg - here is the full evidence and the one interaction that actually matters.

title: Taurine: The Longevity Hype the Follow-Up Studies Walked Back
description: A 2023 mouse study made taurine the it-supplement of biohacking. Two 2025 human studies then found the opposite of what the hype claimed. Here is the real, more boring, more useful story.

title: Akkermansia: The Gut Bacterium That Eats Its Own Protection
description: Akkermansia muciniphila is the fastest-growing name in gut health research, with real human RCTs showing improved insulin sensitivity. It also survives by eating the mucus layer that protects your gut - and too much of it can backfire.

title: Why Most Published Research Findings Are False
description: A famous 2005 paper argued most published research findings are false. Twenty years later, the debate is still alive - including about the paper itself. Here is how small samples, p-hacking, and publication bias distort supplement research, and how to actually read a study.

title: Glutathione: The "0% Absorbed" Claim That Doesn't Hold Up
description: Liposomal glutathione brands love to say standard oral glutathione is 0% absorbed. A 6-month, 54-person RCT found otherwise - at a fraction of the price. Here is what the actual research says, where it genuinely conflicts, and the one real interaction that matters.

title: Urolithin A: Why Most People Can't Get It From Pomegranates Alone
description: Only 12% of people in one study had detectable urolithin A at baseline - your gut bacteria, not your diet, decide whether you make it at all. Most of the human research comes from the company that patented it. Here is the evidence, the conflict of interest, and what actually holds up.

title: Nattokinase: The Blood Pressure Data Is Real, the Cholesterol Story Isn't Settled
description: A meta-analysis of 546 people found nattokinase lowers blood pressure consistently. The same meta-analysis found low doses worsened HDL and LDL cholesterol, while a 3-year trial found zero effect on actual atherosclerosis. Here is the real evidence and the documented bleeding cases worth knowing about.

title: Saffron: The World's Most Faked Spice Also Has Real Mood Data
description: A December 2025 RCT in healthy adults found saffron didn't move the needle on depression, anxiety, or fatigue. Earlier trials in clinically depressed adults found real effects comparable to SSRIs. Both can be true. Here is the evidence, the 20-30% adulteration problem, and how to actually verify what's in the bottle.

title: Peptides: Two Completely Different Categories Wearing the Same Name
description: Collagen peptides in your protein shake and BPC-157 sold as a 'research chemical' online have almost nothing in common except the word peptide. One category has real human trials and clear legal status. The other has a single research group's animal data and a regulatory status that's changing as you read this.

title: What You Actually Need on Ozempic or Wegovy
description: A Cambridge review found only 12 studies on nutrition during GLP-1 treatment - and most lacked standardized methods. One obesity doctor calls muscle-loss fears manufactured; the data shows 20-60% of weight lost can be lean mass. Here is what's actually worth doing about protein, micronutrients, and muscle.

title: Hangover Supplements: What Actually Works (And What's Just Marketing)
description: A marketing site claims a 2024 study proves DHM cuts hangovers by 70%. The actual study found that number nowhere - only one of nine symptoms hit statistical significance, and not even from DHM alone. Here is what the real research says, including why even NAC failed its own 2024 RCT.

title: Methylene Blue: A Century-Old Drug With a Genuine Modern Safety Warning
description: Methylene blue's mitochondrial electron-cycling mechanism is real biochemistry, not hype. Its FDA prescribing information also lists a specific, documented, sometimes fatal interaction with common antidepressants - one that goes well beyond just SSRIs. Here is the real mechanism and the warning worth taking seriously.

title: Inositol: What the Evidence Actually Supports for PCOS
description: International guidelines recommend inositol as a first-line PCOS option. A January 2026 umbrella review of 13 meta-analyses found zero high-quality evidence behind any of it. Both things are true at once. Here is what the data actually shows, dose by dose, outcome by outcome.

title: Zinc: Why Getting the Dose Right Changes Everything
description: Zinc and the common cold have a confusing research history - some trials show real benefit, others show nothing. The actual explanation is dose: below 75mg/day, zinc lozenges uniformly fail. Above it, the effect is consistent and significant. Here is the full picture, including real copper deficiency cases worth knowing about.

title: B Vitamins: Understanding the Methylation Story Everyone Gets Half Right
description: The eight B vitamins work as an interdependent team, not eight separate nutrients - and the methylation cycle is the thread connecting most of them. MTHFR testing has become a popular explanation for everything from fatigue to anxiety, but major medical reviews say routine testing isn't even clinically justified. Here is the real, family-wide picture.

title: B12: What It Actually Fixes (And What It Doesn't)
description: Cyanocobalamin isn't dangerous, methylcobalamin isn't automatically 'used directly' by your cells, and if you're not actually deficient, B12 probably won't do much for your energy or mood.

title: Why Half the Folate Trials Never Had a Chance
description: One of the most-cited depression trials tested two doses of folate in the exact same design - the low dose failed, the high dose worked. Here is the honest, dose-aware picture.

title: B6: Why Two Health Authorities Disagree by a Factor of Eight
description: EFSA says the safe upper limit for B6 is 12mg/day. The US and WHO say 100mg is fine - eight times higher. Neither number rests on solid RCT dose-response data.

title: B1: Why the Expensive Form May Not Beat the Cheap One
description: Cheap thiamine hydrochloride, at a high enough oral dose, reaches blood levels approaching IV administration - undercutting a big chunk of the case for premium derivatives. Meanwhile, a life-threatening deficiency is still badly underdiagnosed.

title: Niacin: The Cholesterol Drug That Stopped Working in 2011
description: AIM-HIGH and HPS2-THRIVE ended niacin's run as a cholesterol drug. A 2024 Cleveland Clinic study now suggests its own breakdown products may promote vascular inflammation.

title: B5: The Acne Cure Built on a Hypothesis Nobody Tested Twice
tags: B5 Pantothenic Acid Vitamins Acne Pantethine
description: A 1995 uncontrolled case series launched the pantothenic acid acne theory. One small RCT since found a real effect - but pantothenic acid itself does nothing for cholesterol; its cousin pantethine is the one with real data.

title: B7: Why a $50 Biotin Supplement Probably Won't Beat a $5 One
description: Biotin deficiency is genuinely rare in healthy people. The evidence for hair, skin, and nail claims is thin and mostly uncontrolled - and the one time high-dose biotin faced a real placebo-controlled trial, it failed.

title: B2: One Vitamin, One Gene Test, Two Completely Different Answers
tags: B2 Riboflavin Vitamins Migraine MTHFR Blood Pressure
description: Riboflavin has real Class II evidence for adult migraine prevention. A specific MTHFR gene variant determines whether it lowers blood pressure by 6-13 mmHg or does nothing at all.

title: AI and Longevity in 2026: What's Actually Real, and What's Still Phase I
tags: AI Longevity Editorial Retro Biosciences Insilico
description: OpenAI and Retro Biosciences achieved a real 50x jump in cell reprogramming efficiency. AI-discovered drugs succeed in Phase I at 80-90% - but show no advantage yet in Phase II, and zero have reached approval.

title: Vegan Supplements in 2026: One Myth Debunked Twice, One Real Win
tags: Vegan Supplements Editorial B12 Creatine Omega-3
description: Spirulina's B12 is mostly inactive pseudo-cobalamin that can worsen your B12 status. The 'vegans get more from creatine' claim didn't replicate. Algae-derived DHA and EPA genuinely work.

title: Colostrum: Why an Anti-Doping Agency and the Actual Data Disagree
description: WADA has warned athletes off colostrum since 2013 over IGF-1 concerns, based on two flawed studies. The broader controlled evidence finds no IGF-1 increase at standard doses - and colostrum's real strength is gut health, not immune hype.

title: Tongkat Ali: The Testosterone Herb With a 63% Fake Product Problem
description: A meta-analysis shows real testosterone effects but flags major publication bias and heterogeneity. A peer-reviewed DNA barcoding study found only 37% of commercial products were genuinely authentic.

title: Fadogia Agrestis: When the Same Study That Found the Benefit Found the Harm
tags: Fadogia Agrestis Testosterone Safety Animal Studies
description: Zero published human trials exist for Fadogia agrestis. The same research group that found a testosterone increase in rats also found testicular toxicity - and Momentous quietly pulled its product in 2023.

title: Sea Moss: The 92-Minerals Claim Nobody Can Actually Source
tags: Sea Moss Iodine Heavy Metals Thyroid
description: The '92 minerals' claim doesn't trace to any real sea moss analysis. Its measured iodine content varies 60-fold between products, and independent testing has found arsenic, lead, and cadmium exceeding safety limits.

title: Boron: Three Health Authorities, Three Different Safety Limits
description: EFSA sets boron's safe limit at 10mg/day, the US says 20mg, the UK says 6mg - all from animal data. The famous testosterone claim comes from a study of just 8 men over one week.

title: Probiotics: Strain-Specific Science Meets a Label-Accuracy Crisis
tags: Probiotics Editorial Strain Specificity Gut Health CFU
description: The strain matters more than the species name. A 2020 meta-analysis found multi-strain formulas aren't more effective than single-strain ones. Only 27% of tested products meet their labeled CFU claim, and a 2025 study confirms some strains delay gut recovery after antibiotics.

title: Electrolytes: The One Real Danger and the Many Overstated Claims
description: Sodium during exercise has minimal evidence for improving performance, and magnesium hasn't reliably prevented cramps. But exercise-associated hyponatremia from overdrinking plain water is a real, occasionally fatal risk that gets far less attention.

title: Apple Cider Vinegar Gummies: A Retracted Study and an Undissolved Tablet Problem
tags: Apple Cider Vinegar Gummies Retraction Weight Loss
description: The headline-grabbing ACV weight loss study was retracted in September 2025. A 2026 lawsuit alleges a popular gummy brand delivers acetic acid below the legal vinegar threshold. Whole vinegar tablets also failed to match liquid vinegar's effects.

title: Lithium Orotate: A Reasonable Microdose Approach, With One Important Exception
tags: Lithium Orotate Brain Health Alzheimer's Bipolar
description: A 2025 Nature mouse study found real neuroprotective effects from lithium orotate. No human trial has tested the orotate form yet. Here is a sensible, low-dose approach - and why anyone on prescribed lithium should never substitute it.

title: Vaginal Probiotics: The Strain-Specific Evidence That Actually Holds Up
tags: Vaginal Probiotics BV Women's Health Strain Specificity
description: Specific strains, added to standard antibiotics, roughly tripled bacterial vaginosis cure rates in randomized trials. Yeast infection evidence is promising but smaller-scale. UTI prevention evidence is genuinely thinner.

title: Creatine Across a Woman's Life: What's Proven, What's Promising
description: A 2025 RCT found 5g/day of creatine plus resistance training improved strength, sleep, and cognition in perimenopausal and postmenopausal women. Here is what's proven and what's still emerging, stage by stage.

title: Melatonin: Why Less Is More, and Why Kids Need Extra Caution
description: A dose-response meta-analysis found melatonin's benefit peaks at 3-5mg, not the 10mg pills on most shelves. Meanwhile, melatonin gummies are now the leading cause of unsupervised pediatric medication ingestion in US ERs.

title: Rhodiola Rosea: The Adaptogen With Real Evidence - If You Get the Right Extract
description: Three independent RCTs found real, objectively measured fatigue and cognitive benefits using the SHR-5 extract. Independent lab testing found up to 80% of commercial products contain far less of the active compounds than labeled.

title: Perimenopause Protocol: One Stack Per Symptom, Evidence-Ranked
tags: Perimenopause Protocol Vitamin D Creatine Black Cohosh
description: Vitamin D and creatine have solid RCT support. Black cohosh and soy isoflavones for hot flashes sit in genuine scientific disagreement. Omega-3 and ashwagandha don't specifically help hot flashes despite marketing claims.

title: Pine Bark Extract: Why the Breadth of Its Claims Is the First Red Flag
tags: Pine Bark Pycnogenol Endothelial Function Cochrane
description: A Cochrane review found insufficient evidence across seven conditions. One small trial found a real, strong endothelial function signal. The breadth of claims made for this extract is itself worth reading as a warning sign.

title: Saw Palmetto: Popular for Prostate Health, But Does It Work?
tags: Saw Palmetto BPH Prostate NEJM Trial
description: Early meta-analyses found real BPH symptom improvement. A landmark year-long NEJM trial and a triple-dose follow-up found no benefit at all. A 2023 review of 27 studies confirms the pattern - here is the full story.

title: Manganese Supplements: Low Risk, Low Reason to Bother
description: Manganese deficiency is essentially theoretical in real populations. A 2024 review found zero supplementation trials for rheumatic disease. The real safety concern is occupational exposure and liver disease, not dietary supplements.

title: OPC (Grape Seed Extract): Why the Studies Used 10x More Than Your Bottle
tags: OPC Grape Seed Extract Proanthocyanidins Pine Bark Comparison
description: Clinical trials used 150-2,000mg/day of grape seed extract; most commercial products contain 50-100mg. NCCIH says evidence is insufficient for any indication. A direct comparison to Pine Bark Extract, with a verdict on which is the better bet.
